ew_context: GBPJPY completing inverse H&S reversal pattern at wave 2 or B wave low, positioning for impulsive advance above neckline at 215.39
pattern_desc: Textbook inverse head and shoulders with head at 214.59 and neckline at 215.39. Right shoulder forming near 215.02 with bullish divergence.
invalidation: Close below 214.89 on H4 timeframe breaks the pattern structure and negates bullish reversal scenario
market_context: GBP showing relative strength amid UK inflation concerns, while JPY weakness on BoJ policy divergence supports the cross higher
full_analysis:
## Market Structure & Technical Context

GBPJPY has formed a precise inverse head and shoulders pattern with the head low at 214.59 and neckline resistance at 215.39. The pattern shows excellent symmetry with left shoulder near 214.71, head at 214.59, and right shoulder forming around 215.02. Current positioning at 215.35 sits just below the critical neckline breakout level, offering an optimal risk-reward entry point.

RSI at 57.3% shows healthy momentum with room for expansion on the breakout. EMA20 at 215.21 has crossed above EMA50 at 215.10, confirming bullish momentum shift and pattern completion. ATR of 0.16 reflects normal volatility for this cross, supporting the tight 2.9x ATR stop placement.

## Key Levels

- **Resistance**: 215.39 (neckline trigger), 216.19 (measured move target)
- **Support**: 215.02 (right shoulder), 214.89 (pattern invalidation)

## Invalidation Level

Close below 214.89 on H4 timeframe would break the inverse head and shoulders structure and signal continuation of the prior downtrend.

## Macro & Flow Context

UK inflation jumping to 3.3% supports GBP strength expectations, while JPY remains under pressure from BoJ policy divergence. CFD momentum alignment confirms institutional positioning for the breakout scenario.
